title: श्री सत्यनारायण आरती
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सत्यनारायण आरती ॥

जय लक्ष्मी रमणा, स्वामी जय लक्ष्मी रमणा।
सत्यनारायण स्वामी, जन पातक हरणा॥

जय लक्ष्मी रमणा॥

रत्न जड़ित सिंहासन, अद्भुत छबि राजे।
नारद कहत निरन्तर, घण्टा घड़ियाल बाजे॥

जय लक्ष्मी रमणा॥

प्रकट भये कलि कारण, द्विज को दरश दियो।
बूढ़ा ब्राह्मण बनकर, कंचन महल कियो॥

जय लक्ष्मी रमणा॥

दुर्बल भील कठारो, जिनपर कृपा करी।
चन्द्रचूड़ एक राजा, जिनकी विपत्ति हरी॥

जय लक्ष्मी रमणा॥

वैश्य मनोरथ पूरण, करो दयानिधि जी।
दीनदयालु दयानिधि, करो कृपा भरी॥

जय लक्ष्मी रमणा॥

श्री सत्यनारायण जी की आरती, जो कोई गावे।
कहत शिवानन्द स्वामी, मनवांछित फल पावे॥

जय लक्ष्मी रमणा॥

॥ इति श्री सत्यनारायण आरती सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Satyanarayan Aarti ||

Jai Lakshmi Ramana, Swami Jai Lakshmi Ramana.
Satyanarayan Swami, Jan Patak Harana.

Jai Lakshmi Ramana.

Ratna Jadit Singhasan, Adbhut Chhabi Raje.
Narad Kahat Nirantar, Ghanta Ghadiyal Baje.

Jai Lakshmi Ramana.

Prakat Bhaye Kali Karan, Dwij Ko Darash Diyo.
Boodha Brahman Bankar, Kanchan Mahal Kiyo.

Jai Lakshmi Ramana.

Durbal Bheel Kathaaro, Jinpar Kripa Kari.
Chandrachud Ek Raja, Jinki Vipatti Hari.

Jai Lakshmi Ramana.

Vaishya Manorath Puran, Karo Dayanidhi Ji.
Deendayalu Dayanidhi, Karo Kripa Bhari.

Jai Lakshmi Ramana.

Shri Satyanarayan Ji Ki Aarti, Jo Koi Gaave.
Kahat Shivanand Swami, Manvanchhit Phal Paave.

Jai Lakshmi Ramana.

|| Iti Shri Satyanarayan Aarti Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
जय हो लक्ष्मी के रमण (विष्णु भगवान) की! हे स्वामी सत्यनारायण! जनों के पापों को हरने वाले!

रत्नजड़ित सिंहासन पर अद्भुत छबि राजित है। नारद निरन्तर कहते हैं, घण्टा-घड़ियाल बजते हैं।

कलियुग के कारण प्रकट हुए, ब्राह्मण को दर्शन दिए। बूढ़े ब्राह्मण का रूप धरकर कंचन महल बनाया।

दुर्बल भील कठारो पर कृपा की। चन्द्रचूड़ नामक राजा की विपत्ति हरी।

हे दयानिधि! वैश्य की मनोकामनाएँ पूरण करो। हे दीनदयालु! भरपूर कृपा करो।

श्री सत्यनारायण जी की यह आरती जो कोई गाता है, शिवानन्द स्वामी कहते हैं — वह मनवांछित फल पाता है।
benefits: Essential aarti for Satyanarayan Puja - one of the most performed Hindu rituals
Invokes Lord Vishnu as Satyanarayan for truthfulness and righteousness
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires and removes all calamities
Traditionally associated with prosperity and blessings of lakshmi to the household
Sung on Purnima (full moon) and all auspicious occasions
description: The Satyanarayan Aarti, known in Sanskrit as श्री सत्यनारायण आरती, is a traditional worship song (aarti) in praise of Lord Vishnu. Drawn from Traditional Aarti Sangrah, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to bring prosperity and blessings of Lakshmi to the household, sung on Purnima (full moon) and all auspicious occasions, and fulfil all desires and remove all calamities. For best results, devotees chant this on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Purnima and Satyanarayan Puja. With 6 verses and a reading time of about 4 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Satyanarayan Aarti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Satyanarayan Aarti in Hindi with meaning. Jai Lakshmi Ramana - essential aarti for Satyanarayan Puja on Purnima and auspicious occasions.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Satyanarayan Aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: The Satyanarayan Aarti, beginning with 'Om Jai Lakshmi Ramana, Swami Jai Lakshmi Ramana,' is the closing aarti of the Satyanarayan Vrat Katha, addressed to Lord Vishnu in his form as Satyanarayan, the Lord of Truth. The aarti praises Vishnu as the consort of Lakshmi (Lakshmi-Ramana), the destroyer of devotees' sins, and the bestower of the boons described in the five chapters of the Satyanarayan Katha narrated in the Reva Khanda of the Skanda Purana. (Source: BhaktiInformation; Times Now Navbharat.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Satyanarayan Aarti sung?
seoFaqs.answer: The aarti is sung at the conclusion of the Satyanarayan Vrat, traditionally observed on Purnima (full moon day) of every Hindu month and especially on Kartik Purnima, Vaishakh Purnima, Margashirsha Purnima, and Sharad Purnima. It is also performed on Ekadashi, Sankranti, and on auspicious life-events such as housewarmings, weddings, anniversaries, and child-naming ceremonies. The vrat involves listening to the five-chapter Satyanarayan Katha, performing puja, and then singing the aarti before distributing sapatpadi prasad. (Source: 99Pandit; Pilgrimage Tour.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the Satyanarayan Vrat Katha contain?
seoFaqs.answer: The Satyanarayan Katha, found in the Reva Khanda of the Skanda Purana, narrates how the vrat was first taught by Lord Narayana to Narada, then by Suta to the sages at Naimisharanya. The five chapters tell stories of devotees from different walks of life, a poor brahmin, a woodcutter, a king, a merchant family, and a group of cowherds, each of whom prospered by performing the vrat and suffered hardship when they neglected it. The aarti is the devotional climax that follows the katha. (Source: Times Now Navbharat; BhaktiInformation.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Vishnu called 'Lakshmi Ramana' in this aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: Lakshmi Ramana literally means 'the beloved of Lakshmi,' an affectionate epithet of Vishnu in his role as the divine consort of the goddess of prosperity. In the Satyanarayan tradition, Vishnu is invoked specifically as the Lord of Truth (Satya-Narayana) who, when worshipped with sincerity, brings both material abundance (Lakshmi's blessings) and spiritual liberation (Narayana's grace). The aarti's choice of this epithet emphasises the wholeness of dharma, artha, and moksha that the vrat promises. (Source: Ramodey; Dharmsaar.)

seoFaqs.question: What prasad is offered during Satyanarayan Aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional prasad is sapatpadi or sapadi, a sweet preparation of equal portions (typically 1.25 measures each) of wheat flour (sooji or atta), sugar, ghee, and ripe banana, sometimes with chopped nuts and tulsi leaves. Other offerings include panchamrit (milk, curd, ghee, honey, sugar), banana, coconut, betel leaves, betel nuts, and a tulsi leaf placed on the prasad. After the aarti, the prasad is distributed first to brahmins and then to all attendees as Satyanarayan kripa. (Source: Pilgrimage Tour; Bhakti Gaane.)
